description:
You need to log the nutritional information for a product by scanning its barcode.

1. A web browser is open on your screen displaying a product barcode.
2. Launch bcWebCam (if not already running) and position/configure it to scan the barcode visible on the screen. (bcWebCam can capture barcodes from the screen if placed over them).
3. Read the decoded 12-digit UPC number.
4. Open the local database at 'C:\workspace\openfoodfacts_subset.csv'. This file contains three columns: code, product_name, and energy-kcal_100g.
5. Find the row matching the scanned UPC and extract its product_name and energy-kcal_100g.
6. Append a single line to the daily diary file at 'C:\workspace\daily_diary.txt'. The line MUST be in exactly this format:
   [UPC] - [product_name] - [energy-kcal_100g] kcal
   (Example: 012345678901 - Sample Product - 100 kcal)

Ensure the final text file is saved correctly.
